rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions To Trustee Aaron Chong; Compel Trustee To Provide Information Render Proper Accounting, And Distribute Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine how best to proceed. This Examiner has been unable to determine why the San Francisco property located at 750-752 39th Avenue will be distributed to Nicholas Chong as the trustee of the House Trust, when Mo Yung Wong Chong's Amendment to the Survivor[']s Trust dated 1/29/10 named Aileen Beth Chong Jeung as the trustee of that trust, then Samantha Jeung, and then Nicholas Chong. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CARL DERINGER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The conservator and attorneys request compensation in the total amount of $12,524 for their services rendered during this account period. The current balance of the conservator's set-aside account pooling the fees previously taken on account is $19,896.25. If the requested amount of compensation of the conservator and attorneys is allowed in full to be paid in a lump sum, the remaining balance of the set-aside account will be $7,372.25. The conservatorship estate only holds cash of $1,445.86. 2) The conservators' attorneys' hourly rate is $350 for this account period, even though the rate was $290 for the prior account period. 3) The account does not show any share of cost payment to the current facility between October 2019 and March 2021. 4) Should the Court withdraw the onservator's to take fees on account, as set out in the February 8, 2018 Order, if the conservatee is only allowed to keep $35 per month from his only income from the VA, and the estate's cash balance is reportedly $1,445? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDNA MARKETON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/03/2022. 1) The court will not grant the petition until a personal representative has been appointed in the estate of Gladys E. Kottschade Holm and letters are filed or the petition is amended to distribute this portion to the county treasurer. 2) The Final I&A alleges $1,599,164.88 in assets. The Summary of Account alleges the total property on hand at beginning of account is $1,599,164.89. Schedule I alleges $1,599,164.89 in assets. 3) The caption of the proposed order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b.] 4) Please explain why notice was given to Walter Ludwig Swododa at a P.O. Box 1660 Yucaipa CA, 92399 when prior notice and his address are listed as 1000 LaLania Avenue Redlands, CA 92374 and the 1000 LaLania Ave address is listed in paragraph 30 of the petition. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 12/20/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LETECIA BARAJAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petition requests an order compelling the heirs individually to take out a loan secured by the estate real property after distribution and to pay statutory fees of the administrator and her attorney and costs. Probate Code section 10831(d) provides in relevant part that: "the court shall make an order fixing and allowing the compensation for all services rendered in the estate proceeding. In the case of an allowance to the personal representative, the order shall authorize the personal representative to charge against the estate the amount allowed. In the case of the attorney's compensation the order shall require the personal representative to pay the attorney out of the estate the amount allowed." 2) The administrator has paid $6,714.08 from her personal funds to satisfy the restitution obligation of one of the heirs who has previously been incarcerated. The administrator requests an order requiring the heir to reimburse the administrator within 120 days after distribution of the estate real property. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE CLAUDE ESMIOL REVOCABLE TRUST DATED NOVEMBER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Assets Of Trust And For Order Transferring Assets To Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/11/22 by the judicial officer for the following issues to be addressed: 1) Why does the petition request that 10.1% ownership in Esmiol Properties, LLC, be confirmed to the trust when the operating agreement shows just a 1.1% interest? The fact that the trust listed just her "interest" in the LLC is not sufficient. 2) Notice needs to be served on Paul Tour-Sarkissian, as the agent for service of Esmiol Properties, LLC, as that is a separate capacity. 3) The trustees need to address how they are able to properly administer the trust and meet their fiduciary duties, given that their attorney apparently has a conflict of interest.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM H. GREEN REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Title To Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/03/2022. 1. Venue is a threshold issue that must be determined prior to the petition. Please explain and provide the legal basis /cite probate code sections for why San Francisco is the proper venue when the trustee appears to live in Edgewood, MD. 2. Please file evidence showing the Wells Fargo bank account ending in 9287 was in existence prior to execution of the trust. 3. Please file a current account statement of the bank account. 4. Please explain all the Decedent's heirs so the court can determine whether notice is proper under Prob. C. 851 (b). 5. The proposed order does not match what is requested in the prayer/request in the petition. It appears the petition must be amended to match what is requested in the proposed order as the prayer/request in the petition does not indicate the account number or where the bank account is and just says "Directing that the Bank Account is an asset of the William H. Green Revocable Trust." Also the second page of the proposed order "That the parties shall execute all documents and perform such additional acts as may be necessary to carry out the above" is not in the petition's prayer/request. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 12/20/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARK ALAN WINKLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/14/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies, no proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 A. and B., and 14.60 E.] Additionally, the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities and the Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number have not yet been filed. In hopes of avoiding a continuance, the petition was skimmed online as a ONE-TIME courtesy. Non-resident personal representatives are required to post bond, even if a will waives bond or all heirs waive bond. A declaration must be filed, setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] The proof of service appears to be incomplete (no date of mailing). Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/3/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
